name: Maxilla
description: Two bones that form the central part of the face and the upper jaw.
Forms the majority of the hard palate, the floor and lateral walls of the nasal cavity, and part of the orbital floor.
Part of the maxilla that extends superiorly to articulate with the frontal and nasal bones.
Forms the medial border of the orbit.
A small opening located just inferior to the orbital rim.
Transmits the infraorbital nerve (branch of CN V2) and blood vessels.
Serves as a landmark for regional anesthesia in dental procedures.
Part of the maxilla that extends laterally and superiorly to articulate with the zygomatic bone.
Helps form the lateral wall of the orbit.
Forms the base of the malar prominence (cheekbone).
The inferiorly projecting portion of the maxilla that contains the upper teeth.
Provides support and attachment for the maxillary teeth.
Resorbs with age and loss of teeth.
